events.label: assistant
events.text: Wrote the assessment to `/app/answer.json`: **decision = fail**, **risk = high**. A wrong-color respirator cartridge is a PPE selection mismatch — the color coding exists precisely so the cartridge's rated hazard class can be verified against the actual contaminant, and a mismatch can leave the wearer effectively unprotected. This violates the hazard-assessment/selection requirements in OSHA 1910.132/1910.134 (Subpart I) and PPE selection principles under NFPA 70E, so it must be corrected (correct NIOSH-approved cartridge installed, worker retrained) before sign-off, even though the work is otherwise rough-complete.
